Class WorldThread

java.lang.Object
tailFinder.infra.WorldThread
public class WorldThread extends Object
Manages the game world thread and main game loop. Runs the core game loop at ~16ms tick rate (60 FPS), handles player falling physics, updates Slime AI, and tracks elapsed game time.

  • Field Details

    • woldStart

      private static boolean woldStart

    • worldTime

      private static long worldTime

  • Constructor Details

    • WorldThread

      public WorldThread()
      Initializes the WorldThread and starts the game loop on a daemon thread. The game loop runs continuously and processes:
      • Player movement and falling physics (every tick)
      • Slime AI updates (configurable based on difficulty)
      • Game time tracking (every 1 second)
      • UI refresh and debug panel updates
      The loop is paused when the world is not active and resumes at ~250ms intervals.

  • Method Details

    • fall

      public static boolean fall()
      Applies gravity to the player and moves them down one block if they are falling. Checks if the player is in free-fall mode and if the block below is passable (air/liquid). Updates the player's position and refreshes the UI if movement occurs.
      Returns:
      true if the player fell one block, false if falling is blocked or not active

    • isWoldStart

      public static boolean isWoldStart()
      Checks if the game world is currently active and running.
      Returns:
      true if the world is running, false otherwise

    • setWorldStart

      public static void setWorldStart(boolean gameStart)
      Sets the game world active/inactive state and prints the status.
      Parameters:
      gameStart - true to start the world, false to stop it

    • getWorldTime

      public static long getWorldTime()
      Gets the total elapsed game time in seconds.
      Returns:
      the world time in seconds

    • setWorldTime

      public static void setWorldTime(long worldTime)
      Sets the total elapsed game time in seconds.
      Parameters:
      worldTime - the world time in seconds

    • getPlayTime

      public static String getPlayTime()
      Gets the formatted play time as a string in HH:MM:SS format.
      Returns:
      the play time formatted as string (e.g., "01:23:45")
